chunks.text: People spend much of their life at work.
An occupation is not merely a way to earn a living.
It is also a major factor that shapes who a person is.
A student graduates from university, applies for a position, and gets hired.
Under a new employer, the person begins working
associating with colleagues and superiors while learning the craft.
If judged not suitable for the work, one may be fired.
Unemployment has a major impact not only on finances but also on mental health.
On the other hand, some people use their talent to start their own enterprise.
A venture involves risk, but it also holds potentially great reward.
More people are changing their profession in the middle of their career.
In an age where people live to be a hundred
there is no need to cling to a single job.
One's priority changes at each stage of life.
What matters is to find meaning in one's work.
Satisfaction lies not in the size of a paycheck
but in the feeling that one is contributing to society.
